page: Amazon Web Services i7i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, which correspond to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a base speed of 2.4 GHz. The processor is supported by a large 320 MB L3 cache and includes advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled directly with the instance, consisting of a 3750 GB local NVMe SSD, while network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 9 Gbps managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities in memory and single-threaded operations. The server achieves top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for single-threaded gzip compression at 34.75 GB/sec, Geekbench single-core scoring at 2308.0, and Geekbench text processing. It also demonstrates strong write memory bandwidth for large blocks at 91.67 GB/sec and a high PassMark memory score. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET operations fall within the average range. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which assist in cryptographic and vector calculations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7i.4xlarge offers high resource density by combining 128 GB of DDR5 RAM with a substantial 3.75 TB local NVMe SSD, reducing the need for external block storage attachments. This hardware profile is well-suited for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads. Appropriate use cases include relational and non-relational databases, caching layers, data analytics, and text processing applications that benefit from high local read/write speeds and dedicated CPU allocation.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz and a bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing and gzip compression, alongside strong memory write bandwidth. The hardware configuration, which lacks a GPU, is optimized for high-throughput data environments. It is highly suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR5 memory, and a high-capacity local NVMe SSD.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors
128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance
9 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for databases, caching layers, and data-intensive workloads
